intro: Glechoma kokamirica, also known as Kokamirica, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Glechoma kokamirica include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Glechoma kokamirica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
